home.features.description.desc.message: The central metadata — text, function or i18n ID.

home.features.annotation.desc.message: endpoint, title, params and responses in samples 01–02.

components.betaDisclaimer.body.message: The REST-DOC module is recent in tlppCore and still under implementation. The generation engine is property of TOTVS S.A. and is not under the MIT license. Open an issue in the repository for questions.

components.virtualBanner.message: Virtual documentation — .tlpp examples run on your Onça Preta AppServer, not in this site.

home.features.basic01.desc.message: The simplest path: a string in the @Get annotation.

home.features.basic02.desc.message: title, params and responses directly in the REST annotation.

home.features.i18n.desc.message: Documentation via i18n ID or function returning JSON.

home.features.complete.desc.message: REST + DOC + components + localization in a single service.

home.features.dynamic.desc.message: REST created via JSON, documented with tlpp.doc.List().

home.features.api.desc.message: Paste or upload your .yaml and browse endpoints.

home.card.rest.backDesc.message: Full documentation for the native tlppCore REST API — from server setup to production-ready examples.

home.card.rest.highlight1.message: Guided path from basics to advanced

home.card.docGenerate.backDesc.message: Learn to document APIs in TLPP code and generate OpenAPI specs automatically.

home.card.github.backDesc.message: Open-source code to study, clone and adapt in your TLPP projects.

home.card.tools.backDesc.message: Interactive utilities to speed up development and validate APIs right in the browser.

home.card.tools.highlight3.message: No install — runs in the browser

home.card.rest.desc.message: Server, routes, oREST, authentication, callbacks and examples on the AppServer.

home.card.docGenerate.desc.message: In-code metadata, tlpp.doc.generate() and OpenAPI output.

home.card.github.desc.message: Repositories with TLPP sample code for REST, metadata and related projects.

home.card.tools.desc.message: Browser tools — OpenAPI explorer and utilities for TLPP.

yamlViewer.subtitle.message: Paste or upload YAML from tlpp.doc.generate(), browse endpoints, and send real requests to your AppServer.

yamlViewer.dropHint.message: Drag a .yaml here or click to select

yamlViewer.empty.message: Paste YAML, upload a file, or load the repository sample to get started.

yamlViewer.baseUrlHint.message: Use HTTP (e.g. http://localhost:8080). With npm start and the AppServer on the same machine, this usually works without extra setup.

yamlViewer.try.bodyHint.message: Leave empty on GET when there is no request body

tools.lead.message: Browser tools to explore OpenAPI documentation and speed up TLPP development — without a local AppServer.

tools.openapi.description.message: Paste or upload YAML from tlpp.doc.generate() and browse endpoints, parameters, and responses — no AppServer required.
